There is no information found on the specific plant species "Rockhausenia villosa". However, I can provide information on a plant that is sometimes referred to with the name "Villose" (which refers to having a hairy or villous appearance). For example, the plant Villose is sometimes used to describe various species of herbs in the genus Astragalus or the plant Astragalus membranaceus.

For the sake of providing information on a plant that might match the description, I'll use Astragalus membranaceus.

Astragalus membranaceus, also known as Huang Qi or Astragalus root, is a plant in the pea family (Fabaceae) that has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Astragalus membranaceus include immune system enhancement, anti-inflammatory effects, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus membranaceus include flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Astragalus membranaceus is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
